changes.value:
## What

The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the project folder name (parent of `.kanban`) — not prefixed with "Switch to Board:". Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say "Switch to Board: my-project" while the menu just says "my-project".

### Changes

**1. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
- Add `pub menu_name: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`
- This is the display text used in native menus when present; falls back to `name` when `None`

**2. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
- Change `name: cmd.name.clone()` to `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`
- This applies to ALL menu items — any YAML command can now specify a shorter menu caption

**3. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
- After the static Window menu items (line 166), before minimize/maximize (line 167):
- Read `ui_state.open_boards()` to get board paths
- For each board path, derive the project name: `Path::new(path).parent().and_then(|p| p.file_name())` — same logic as `list_commands_for_scope` in commands.rs
- Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = project name (no "Switch to" prefix)
- Add a separator before the group, and a separator after
- These items dispatch through the existing `board.switch:*` interception in `dispatch_command_internal`

**4. Rebuild menu on board changes** — `rebuild_menu` is already called after `BoardSwitch` and `BoardClose` results (commands.rs:1181-1186), so the Window menu will update when boards open/close.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Window menu shows open boards by project folder name (parent of `.kanban`), in a group between static items and minimize/maximize
- [ ] Clicking a board name in the Window menu switches to that board
- [ ] Palette still shows "Switch to Board: my-project" (full name with prefix)
- [ ] Any YAML command can specify `menu_name` for a shorter menu label
- [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes

## Tests
- [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes from YAML correctly
- [ ] `menu.rs` or `commands.rs` — test: board switch menu items use parent directory name, not `.kanban`
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` passes
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes
Add menu_name to CommandDef and show board switch commands in Window menu

Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):\n- After the static Window menu items (line 166), before minimize/maximize (line 167):\n- Read `ui_state.open_boards()` to get board paths\n- For each board path, derive the project name: `Path::new(path).parent().and_then(|p| p.file_name())` — same logic as `list_commands_for_scope` in commands.rs\n- Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = project name (no \"Switch to\" prefix)\n- Add a separator before the group, and a separator after\n- These items dispatch through the existing `board.switch:*` interception in `dispatch_command_internal`\n\n**4. Rebuild menu on board changes** — `rebuild_menu` is already called after `BoardSwitch` and `BoardClose` results (commands.rs:1181-1186), so the Window menu will update when boards open/close.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Window menu shows open boards by project folder name (parent of `.kanban`), in a group between static items and minimize/maximize\n- [ ] Clicking a board name in the Window menu switches to that board\n- [ ] Palette still shows \"Switch to Board: my-project\" (full name with prefix)\n- [ ] Any YAML command can specify `menu_name` for a shorter menu label\n- [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes\n\n## Tests\n- [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes from YAML correctly\n- [ ] `menu.rs` or `commands.rs` — test: board switch menu items use parent directory name, not `.kanban`\n- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` passes\n- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes

+The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the board's real name — not a path segment, not \"Switch to Board:\". Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say \"Switch to Board: My Project\" while the menu just says \"My Project\".
+
+### Board name resolution (non-hacky)
+
+The board's actual name is already stored in `UIState.recent_boards` as `RecentBoard.name`. This is populated from the board entity's `name` field when opened (`kanban-app/src/state.rs:346-349` reads `ectx.read(\"board\", \"board\").get_str(\"name\")` then calls `touch_recent(path, board_name)`). Use this instead of parsing path segments.
+
+- `build_menu_from_commands` already receives `recent: &[RecentBoard]` — look up board name by path
+- `list_commands_for_scope` in `commands.rs:1353-1369` — look up from `state.ui_state.recent_boards()` instead of `Path::new(path).file_name()`
+
+### Changes
+
+**1. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
+- Add `pub menu_name: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]`
+- This is the display text used in native menus when present; falls back to `name` when `None`
+
+**2. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
+- Change `name: cmd.name.clone()` to `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`
+
+**3. Fix board name in dynamic commands** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1353-1369`):
+- Look up the board name from `state.ui_state.recent_boards()` by matching path, instead of parsing filesystem path segments
+- Fallback to parent directory name if not in recent_boards
+
+**4. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
+- After the static Window menu items (line 166), before minimize/maximize (line 167):
+- Read `ui_state.open_boards()` to get board paths
+- For each board path, look up the real name from `recent` (the `&[RecentBoard]` param already passed to `build_menu_from_commands`)
+- Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = board's real name (no prefix)
+- Add a separator before the group
+- These items dispatch through the existing `board.switch:*` interception in `dispatch_command_internal`
+
+**5. Rebuild menu on board changes** — `rebuild_menu` is already called after `BoardSwitch` and `BoardClose` results (commands.rs:1181-1186), so the Window menu will update automatically.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Window menu shows open boards by their real board name (from the board entity, via recent_boards), in a group between static items and minimize/maximize
+- [ ] Clicking a board name in the Window menu switches to that board
+- [ ] Palette still shows \"Switch to Board: My Project\" (full name with prefix)
+- [ ] Any YAML command can specify `menu_name` for a shorter menu label
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes from YAML correctly
+- [ ] `commands.rs` — test: dynamic board commands use board entity name from recent_boards, not path parsing
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` passes
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,46 +1,52 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the board's real name — not a path segment, not \"Switch to Board:\". Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say \"Switch to Board: My Project\" while the menu just says \"My Project\".
+The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the board's display name. Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say \"Switch to Board: My Project\" while the menu just says \"My Project (my-folder)\".

-### Board name resolution (non-hacky)
+### Board name: `KanbanContext::display_name()`

-The board's actual name is already stored in `UIState.recent_boards` as `RecentBoard.name`. This is populated from the board entity's `name` field when opened (`kanban-app/src/state.rs:346-349` reads `ectx.read(\"board\", \"board\").get_str(\"name\")` then calls `touch_recent(path, board_name)`). Use this instead of parsing path segments.
+The board entity is just an entity — it has a `name` field like any other. The `KanbanContext` owns both the entity data (via `entity_context()`) and the storage path (via `root()`). Add a `display_name()` method on `KanbanContext` that:
+- Reads the board entity's `name` field: `entity_context().read(\"board\", \"board\").get_str(\"name\")`
+- Gets the path stem: `root().parent().file_name()` (the directory above `.kanban`)
+- If name exists: returns `\"{name} ({path_stem})\"` — e.g. \"My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"
+- If no name: returns just the path stem — e.g. \"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"

-- `build_menu_from_commands` already receives `recent: &[RecentBoard]` — look up board name by path
-- `list_commands_for_scope` in `commands.rs:1353-1369` — look up from `state.ui_state.recent_boards()` instead of `Path::new(path).file_name()`
+This replaces ALL ad-hoc path parsing for board names:
+- `commands.rs:1353-1369` (`list_commands_for_scope` BoardInfo) — call `handle.ctx.display_name().await`
+- `state.rs:346-349` (touch_recent) — call `handle.ctx.display_name().await` instead of manual entity read
+- `menu.rs` (Window menu board items) — call `handle.ctx.display_name()` via the board handles

 ### Changes

-**1. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
+**1. Add `display_name()` to `KanbanContext`** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`):
+- `pub async fn display_name(&self) -> String`
+
+**2. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
 - Add `pub menu_name: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]`
-- This is the display text used in native menus when present; falls back to `name` when `None`
+- Display text used in native menus when present; falls back to `name` when `None`

-**2. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
-- Change `name: cmd.name.clone()` to `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`
+**3. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
+- `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`

-**3. Fix board name in dynamic commands** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1353-1369`):
-- Look up the board name from `state.ui_state.recent_boards()` by matching path, instead of parsing filesystem path segments
-- Fallback to parent directory name if not in recent_boards
+**4. Fix board name in dynamic commands** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1353-1369`):
+- Use `handle.ctx.display_name().await` for `BoardInfo.name` instead of path parsing

-**4. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
-- After the static Window menu items (line 166), before minimize/maximize (line 167):
-- Read `ui_state.open_boards()` to get board paths
-- For each board path, look up the real name from `recent` (the `&[RecentBoard]` param already passed to `build_menu_from_commands`)
-- Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = board's real name (no prefix)
-- Add a separator before the group
-- These items dispatch through the existing `board.switch:*` interception in `dispatch_command_internal`
-
-**5. Rebuild menu on board changes** — `rebuild_menu` is already called after `BoardSwitch` and `BoardClose` results (commands.rs:1181-1186), so the Window menu will update automatically.
+**5. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
+- After static Window items, before minimize/maximize
+- For each open board, get its display name from the board handle
+- Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = display name (no \"Switch to\" prefix)
+- Add separator before the group

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Window menu shows open boards by their real board name (from the board entity, via recent_boards), in a group between static items and minimize/maximize
-- [ ] Clicking a board name in the Window menu switches to that board
-- [ ] Palette still shows \"Switch to Board: My Project\" (full name with prefix)
-- [ ] Any YAML command can specify `menu_name` for a shorter menu label
+- [ ] `KanbanContext::display_name()` returns \"{name} ({path_stem})\" or just path_stem
+- [ ] Window menu shows open boards by display name in a group
+- [ ] Clicking a board in Window menu switches to it
+- [ ] Palette still shows \"Switch to Board: {display_name}\"
+- [ ] Any YAML command can specify `menu_name` for shorter menu label
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes from YAML correctly
-- [ ] `commands.rs` — test: dynamic board commands use board entity name from recent_boards, not path parsing
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` passes
+- [ ] `context.rs` — test: `display_name()` returns \"{name} ({path_stem})\" when board entity has a name
+- [ ] `context.rs` — test: `display_name()` returns just path_stem when no name field
+- [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes correctly
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes
\ No newline at end of file

-**3. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,36 +1,43 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the board's display name. Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say \"Switch to Board: My Project\" while the menu just says \"My Project (my-folder)\".
+The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the board's display name. Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say \"Switch to Board: My Project (my-folder)\" while the menu just says \"My Project (my-folder)\".

-### Board name: `KanbanContext::display_name()`
+### Naming hierarchy

-The board entity is just an entity — it has a `name` field like any other. The `KanbanContext` owns both the entity data (via `entity_context()`) and the storage path (via `root()`). Add a `display_name()` method on `KanbanContext` that:
-- Reads the board entity's `name` field: `entity_context().read(\"board\", \"board\").get_str(\"name\")`
-- Gets the path stem: `root().parent().file_name()` (the directory above `.kanban`)
-- If name exists: returns `\"{name} ({path_stem})\"` — e.g. \"My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"
-- If no name: returns just the path stem — e.g. \"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"
+Two layers, composed at point of use:

-This replaces ALL ad-hoc path parsing for board names:
-- `commands.rs:1353-1369` (`list_commands_for_scope` BoardInfo) — call `handle.ctx.display_name().await`
-- `state.rs:346-349` (touch_recent) — call `handle.ctx.display_name().await` instead of manual entity read
-- `menu.rs` (Window menu board items) — call `handle.ctx.display_name()` via the board handles
+1. **`KanbanContext::name()`** — pure path-derived, synchronous, no entity reads:
+   - `root().parent().file_name()` → e.g. `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`
+   - This is the context's identity from where it lives on disk

+2. **Board display name** — composed from the board entity's `name` field + the context name:
+   - `\"{board_entity.name} ({context.name()})\"` → e.g. `\"My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"`
+   - If no board entity name: just `context.name()` → e.g. `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`
+   - Assembled by the caller (menu builder, palette, etc.) who has access to both
+
+The context doesn't read the entity. The entity doesn't know about the context.
+
 ### Changes

-**1. Add `display_name()` to `KanbanContext`** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`):
-- `pub async fn display_name(&self) -> String`
+**1. Add `name()` to `KanbanContext`** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`):
+- `pub fn name(&self) -> &str` — returns path stem above `.kanban`, synchronous
+- Compute once in constructor, store as field
+
+**2. Add helper to build board display name** (`kanban-app/src/state.rs` or a shared util):
+- Takes a `&KanbanContext` and reads the board entity to compose `\"{name} ({ctx.name()})\"` or just `ctx.name()`
+- Used by `state.rs` (touch_recent), `commands.rs` (BoardInfo), `menu.rs` (Window menu items)

-**2. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
+**3. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
 - Add `pub menu_name: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]`
 - Display text used in native menus when present; falls back to `name` when `None`

-**3. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
+**4. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
 - `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`

-**4. Fix board name in dynamic commands** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1353-1369`):
-- Use `handle.ctx.display_name().await` for `BoardInfo.name` instead of path parsing
+**5. Fix board name in dynamic commands** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1353-1369`):
+- Use the board display name helper for `BoardInfo.name` instead of path parsing

-**5. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
+**6. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
 - After static Window items, before minimize/maximize
 - For each open board, get its display name from the board handle
 - Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = display name (no \"Switch to\" prefix)
@@ -37,16 +44,17 @@
 - Add separator before the group

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `KanbanContext::display_name()` returns \"{name} ({path_stem})\" or just path_stem
+- [ ] `KanbanContext::name()` returns the path stem above `.kanban`
+- [ ] Board display name shows `\"{name} ({context_name})\"` when entity has a name, just context name otherwise
 - [ ] Window menu shows open boards by display name in a group
 - [ ] Clicking a board in Window menu switches to it
-- [ ] Palette still shows \"Switch to Board: {display_name}\"
-- [ ] Any YAML command can specify `menu_name` for shorter menu label
+- [ ] Palette shows \"Switch to Board: {display_name}\"
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `context.rs` — test: `display_name()` returns \"{name} ({path_stem})\" when board entity has a name
-- [ ] `context.rs` — test: `display_name()` returns just path_stem when no name field
+- [ ] `context.rs` — test: `name()` returns correct path stem
+- [ ] `state.rs` or util — test: board display name with entity name returns `\"{name} ({stem})\"`
+- [ ] `state.rs` or util — test: board display name without entity name returns just stem

 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,60 +1,62 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands grouped together, showing the board's display name. Commands need a separate `menu_name` field so the palette can say \"Switch to Board: My Project (my-folder)\" while the menu just says \"My Project (my-folder)\".
+The Window menu should dynamically include board switch commands, and command names should use templates like `{{entity.display_name}}` instead of hardcoded format strings. This extends the existing `{{entity.type}}` template system.

-### Naming hierarchy
+### Template variables

-Two layers, composed at point of use:
+Extend `resolve_name_template` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` to support:

-1. **`KanbanContext::name()`** — pure path-derived, synchronous, no entity reads:
-   - `root().parent().file_name()` → e.g. `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`
-   - This is the context's identity from where it lives on disk
+| Template | Resolves to | Example |
+|---|---|---|
+| `{{entity.type}}` | Capitalized entity type (existing) | `\"Task\"` |
+| `{{entity.display_name}}` | Entity name + context name | `\"My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"` |
+| `{{entity.context.name}}` | Context path stem | `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"` |
+
+`{{entity.display_name}}` is composed: if the entity has a `name` field, returns `\"{name} ({context.name})\"`; if not, returns just `context.name`.

-2. **Board display name** — composed from the board entity's `name` field + the context name:
-   - `\"{board_entity.name} ({context.name()})\"` → e.g. `\"My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"`
-   - If no board entity name: just `context.name()` → e.g. `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`
-   - Assembled by the caller (menu builder, palette, etc.) who has access to both
+### Naming hierarchy (unchanged)

-The context doesn't read the entity. The entity doesn't know about the context.
+1. **`KanbanContext::name()`** — sync, path-derived: `root().parent().file_name()` → `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`. Stored as field.
+2. **Board display name** — composed at template resolution time from entity `name` field + `context.name()`.

 ### Changes

 **1. Add `name()` to `KanbanContext`** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`):
-- `pub fn name(&self) -> &str` — returns path stem above `.kanban`, synchronous
-- Compute once in constructor, store as field
+- `pub fn name(&self) -> &str` — returns path stem above `.kanban`, synchronous, computed once

-**2. Add helper to build board display name** (`kanban-app/src/state.rs` or a shared util):
-- Takes a `&KanbanContext` and reads the board entity to compose `\"{name} ({ctx.name()})\"` or just `ctx.name()`
-- Used by `state.rs` (touch_recent), `commands.rs` (BoardInfo), `menu.rs` (Window menu items)
+**2. Extend template resolution** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`):
+- Expand `resolve_name_template` signature to accept optional entity name and context name
+- Support `{{entity.display_name}}` and `{{entity.context.name}}` in addition to `{{entity.type}}`
+- Dynamic board commands use templates: name = `\"Switch to Board: {{entity.display_name}}\"`, menu_name = `\"{{entity.display_name}}\"`

-**3. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs:66-84`):
-- Add `pub menu_name: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]`
-- Display text used in native menus when present; falls back to `name` when `None`
+**3. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs`):
+- `pub menu_name: Option<String>` — display text for native menus, falls back to `name`

 **4. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
 - `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`

-**5. Fix board name in dynamic commands** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1353-1369`):
-- Use the board display name helper for `BoardInfo.name` instead of path parsing
+**5. Pass context/entity data into dynamic command generation** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs`):
+- `BoardInfo` gains a `context_name` field (from `handle.ctx.name()`) and `entity_name` field (from board entity's `name`)
+- `commands_for_scope` uses these to resolve templates in dynamic command names

-**6. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:150-189`):
+**6. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs`):
 - After static Window items, before minimize/maximize
-- For each open board, get its display name from the board handle
-- Create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label = display name (no \"Switch to\" prefix)
+- For each open board, create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label from resolved template
 - Add separator before the group

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `KanbanContext::name()` returns the path stem above `.kanban`
-- [ ] Board display name shows `\"{name} ({context_name})\"` when entity has a name, just context name otherwise
+- [ ] `{{entity.display_name}}` resolves to `\"{name} ({context_name})\"` or just context name
+- [ ] `{{entity.context.name}}` resolves to the context path stem
 - [ ] Window menu shows open boards by display name in a group
-- [ ] Clicking a board in Window menu switches to it
-- [ ] Palette shows \"Switch to Board: {display_name}\"
+- [ ] Palette shows \"Switch to Board: My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `context.rs` — test: `name()` returns correct path stem
-- [ ] `state.rs` or util — test: board display name with entity name returns `\"{name} ({stem})\"`
-- [ ] `state.rs` or util — test: board display name without entity name returns just stem
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.display_name}}` with name+context → `\"My Project (my-folder)\"`
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.display_name}}` without name → just context name
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.context.name}}` → context name
 - [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes correctly
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes
\ No newline at end of file

-| `{{entity.context.name}}` | Context path stem | `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"` |

+| `{{entity.context.display_name}}` | `KanbanContext::name()` (path stem above `.kanban`) | empty string |

-`{{entity.display_name}}` is composed: if the entity has a `name` field, returns `\"{name} ({context.name})\"`; if not, returns just `context.name`.
+**Simple substitution, no magic.** Each variable resolves independently. Missing = empty string. The template controls the format.

-### Naming hierarchy (unchanged)
+Example board switch command templates:
+- `name: \"Switch to Board: {{entity.display_name}} ({{entity.context.display_name}})\"` → palette: \"Switch to Board: My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"
+- `menu_name: \"{{entity.context.display_name}}\"` → Window menu: \"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"
+
+### Naming hierarchy

-1. **`KanbanContext::name()`** — sync, path-derived: `root().parent().file_name()` → `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`. Stored as field.
-2. **Board display name** — composed at template resolution time from entity `name` field + `context.name()`.
+1. **`KanbanContext::name()`** — sync, path-derived: `root().parent().file_name()` → `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"`. Stored as field, computed once.
+2. **Entity display name** — just the entity's `name` field value. Empty string if not set.
+3. **Composition** — the template controls how these are combined. No implicit formatting.

 ### Changes

 **1. Add `name()` to `KanbanContext`** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`):
-- `pub fn name(&self) -> &str` — returns path stem above `.kanban`, synchronous, computed once
+- `pub fn name(&self) -> &str` — returns path stem above `.kanban`, synchronous, computed once in constructor

 **2. Extend template resolution** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`):
-- Expand `resolve_name_template` signature to accept optional entity name and context name
-- Support `{{entity.display_name}}` and `{{entity.context.name}}` in addition to `{{entity.type}}`
-- Dynamic board commands use templates: name = `\"Switch to Board: {{entity.display_name}}\"`, menu_name = `\"{{entity.display_name}}\"`
+- Expand `resolve_name_template` to accept optional `entity_name: &str` and `context_name: &str`
+- `{{entity.display_name}}` → `entity_name` (empty string if not provided)
+- `{{entity.context.display_name}}` → `context_name` (empty string if not provided)
+- `{{entity.type}}` → capitalized entity type (unchanged)

 **3. Add `menu_name` to `CommandDef`** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/src/types.rs`):
 - `pub menu_name: Option<String>` — display text for native menus, falls back to `name`
+- Also a template — resolved the same way as `name`

 **4. Use `menu_name` in menu builder** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs:59`):
 - `name: cmd.menu_name.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| cmd.name.clone())`

 **5. Pass context/entity data into dynamic command generation** (`kanban-app/src/commands.rs`):
-- `BoardInfo` gains a `context_name` field (from `handle.ctx.name()`) and `entity_name` field (from board entity's `name`)
-- `commands_for_scope` uses these to resolve templates in dynamic command names
+- `BoardInfo` gains `context_name: String` (from `handle.ctx.name()`) and `entity_name: String` (from board entity's `name` field, or empty)
+- `commands_for_scope` passes these through to template resolution

 **6. Add dynamic board switch items to Window menu** (`kanban-app/src/menu.rs`):
 - After static Window items, before minimize/maximize
-- For each open board, create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label from resolved template
+- For each open board, create `MenuItem` with id `board.switch:{path}` and label from resolved `menu_name` template
 - Add separator before the group

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `KanbanContext::name()` returns the path stem above `.kanban`
-- [ ] `{{entity.display_name}}` resolves to `\"{name} ({context_name})\"` or just context name
-- [ ] `{{entity.context.name}}` resolves to the context path stem
-- [ ] Window menu shows open boards by display name in a group
+- [ ] `{{entity.display_name}}` resolves to entity name or empty string
+- [ ] `{{entity.context.display_name}}` resolves to context path stem or empty string
+- [ ] Window menu shows open boards in a group
 - [ ] Palette shows \"Switch to Board: My Project (swissarmyhammer-kanban)\"
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run` passes

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `context.rs` — test: `name()` returns correct path stem
-- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.display_name}}` with name+context → `\"My Project (my-folder)\"`
-- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.display_name}}` without name → just context name
-- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.context.name}}` → context name
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.display_name}}` with name → entity name
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.display_name}}` without name → empty string
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: `{{entity.context.display_name}}` → context name
+- [ ] `scope_commands.rs` — test: combined template resolves both variables
 - [ ] `types.rs` — test: CommandDef with `menu_name` deserializes correctly
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` passes
\ No newline at end of file
